observed_active_context_state.latest_completed_phase: PURG-04 Track A Pointer Residual Repair Patch Packet

observed_decision_locks_latest_packet.packet_title: PURG04 Project_ARIS Main Moved Review Packet

contradictions: ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json, BOOT.md, CURRENT_STATE.md, NEXT_ACTION.md, ROADMAP_CANONICAL.md Active Route, and the latest DECISION_LOCKS packet all point to PURG04_TRACK_A_PATCH_REVIEW_AND_MERGE_DECISION as the live review gate.
ROADMAP_CANONICAL.md Transition Table does not model PURG04_TRACK_A_PATCH_REVIEW_AND_MERGE_DECISION and instead routes PURG-04/pass directly to PURG04_TRACK_A_MAIN_MERGE_EXECUTION.

A_REJECT_MERGE_AND_REQUEST_NEW_TRACK_A_R.summary: Reject the current merge path and require a new cleanroom rebase-review packet against the moved Project_ARIS main.
A_REJECT_MERGE_AND_REQUEST_NEW_TRACK_A_R.technical_effect: Forces a new patch lineage review from origin/main=7c0dc8d14fcbc2bc4246282c7ebf8b0896622dad before any merge authorization is reconsidered.
A_REJECT_MERGE_AND_REQUEST_NEW_TRACK_A_R.tradeoff: Safest for lineage strictness, but it increases cycle cost and is not justified by overlap evidence alone because the patch-vs-current-main delta did not touch forbidden surfaces.

B_AUTHORIZE_REVIEW_ONLY_DIFF_OF_ACTIVE_C.summary: Authorize a bounded review-only diff packet focused on the ACTIVE_CONTEXT_POINTER.md change that moved on Project_ARIS main.
B_AUTHORIZE_REVIEW_ONLY_DIFF_OF_ACTIVE_C.technical_effect: Keeps all mutations blocked while letting the operator isolate whether the forbidden-surface movement is acceptable as external context-pointer drift only.
B_AUTHORIZE_REVIEW_ONLY_DIFF_OF_ACTIVE_C.tradeoff: Minimal and safe, but by itself it does not produce a merge path or bounded integration plan for the Track A patch.

C_AUTHORIZE_NEW_CLEANROOM_MERGE_PLAN_PAC.summary: Authorize a new cleanroom merge-plan packet that excludes ACTIVE_CONTEXT_POINTER.md and any other forbidden surface from the future merge path.
C_AUTHORIZE_NEW_CLEANROOM_MERGE_PLAN_PAC.technical_effect: Preserves review-only mode now, respects the blocker, and creates the only bounded path that can later reconcile the moved main branch with the Track A patch without touching forbidden surfaces.
C_AUTHORIZE_NEW_CLEANROOM_MERGE_PLAN_PAC.tradeoff: Requires another governance packet before any execution, but avoids unnecessary abort or immediate rebase while keeping the route fail-closed.

D_ABORT_TRACK_A_MAIN_MERGE_AND_RETURN_TO.summary: Abort the main-merge path and return the workstream to split-track handling under PURG-04.
D_ABORT_TRACK_A_MAIN_MERGE_AND_RETURN_TO.technical_effect: Stops any attempt to integrate the current Track A patch into Project_ARIS main and reopens broader track selection.
D_ABORT_TRACK_A_MAIN_MERGE_AND_RETURN_TO.tradeoff: Most conservative, but it discards a bounded cleanroom patch that is still internally green and non-overlapping with the forbidden-surface movement.

recommended_option.technical_justification: The blocker is real because Project_ARIS main moved on a forbidden surface, so merge and rebase must remain unauthorized now. At the same time, the recorded review packet shows no Track A overlap with the main movement and no forbidden-surface touch inside the patch itself. A new cleanroom merge-plan packet is therefore the narrowest admissible next move: it keeps review-only governance, preserves the current origin/main pointer state, explicitly excludes ACTIVE_CONTEXT_POINTER.md, and avoids inventing execution authority while the Transition Table still lacks an explicit row for PURG04_TRACK_A_PATCH_REVIEW_AND_MERGE_DECISION.

artifact_sha256.self_hash_limitation: The terminal file sha256 is reported externally after write because a file cannot embed its own final sha256 without self-reference drift.
